channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: CISA Warns Fortinet Customers as FortiBleed Hits 86,644 FortiGate Devices
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday urged Fortinet customers with FortiGate appliances to take steps to secure against ongoing malicious activity aimed at thousands of internet-accessible devices.
The sweeping campaign, believed to be the work of Russian-speaking threat actors, has been codenamed FortiBleed. The number of compromised devices stands at
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: From Assistive to Agentic: The AI Shift That's Redefining Threat Management
item.description:
Introduction
The average enterprise security team has 40 or more security tools, giving a lot of visibility into internal telemetry and asset data. But often, these tools are working in siloes, generating (overlapping) alerts and data. And yet, breach dwell times remain stubbornly long (~43 days), response windows keep closing before teams can act, and analysts burn out triaging noise instead
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CryptoBandits Malware Doubles as a Backdoor, Abuses Tor
item.description: <p>CryptoBandits uses a local SOCKS5 proxy for traffic routing, blending data theft with remote code execution.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cryptobandits-malware-doubles-as-a-backdoor-abuses-tor/">CryptoBandits Malware Doubles as a Backdoor, Abuses Tor</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Anthropic’s Fable and the State of AI
item.description: <p>On June 9th, Anthropic <a href="https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-fable-5-mythos-5">released</a> its Fable generative AI model. Three days later, the US government <a href="https://www.explainx.ai/blog/us-government-bans-fable-5-mythos-5-anthropic-export-control-2026">classified</a> it as a dangerous munition, and used its export-control authority to <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jun/13/anthropic-disable-advanced-ai-models-us-government-order">prohibit</a> any foreign nationals from accessing it. Unable to differentiate between Americans and foreigners, the company <a href="https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access">shut off</a> access for everyone.</p> <p>The government&#8217;s actions <a href="https://freefable.org/">won&#8217;t help</a>. The problem isn&#8217;t any one particular model; it&#8217;s the general trend of increasing AI capabilities. And any real solution requires the sort of collective action that just isn&#8217;t possible right now...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: AWS Unveils 'Continuum,' an AI-Powered Vulnerability Management Platform
item.description: Working with frontier AI models, this new platform aims to help discovering, prioritizing, validating and remediating code vulnerabilities

item.title: FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised
item.description: <p>The large-scale credential theft campaign hit roughly half of the internet-accessible Fortinet firewalls and VPNs.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/fortibleed-86000-fortinet-device-credentials-compromised/">FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Forget Data Leakage: Shadow AI's Real Threat Is Access Control
item.description:
The first wave of enterprise AI concern was straightforward. It was simply employees pasting sensitive data into public AI tools. Security teams responded with usage policies, domain blocks, and data loss prevention rules. That response made sense at the time.
It doesn't fit the problem anymore.
Shadow AI has shifted from a data leakage concern to an access control problem. The threat isn't
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Operation Endgame Disrupts Malware Network Linked to Major Ransomware Gang
item.description: SocGholish malware has been removed from 15,000 sites associated with Evil Corp hackers

item.title: Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack
item.description: <p>The hackers exfiltrated data from Salesforce instances of Klue customers, such as Huntress and Recorded Future.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cybersecurity-firms-impacted-by-klue-supply-chain-attack/">Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
item.description:
Salesforce has revealed that it disabled the Klue Battlecards app integration within its platform in response to a security incident impacting the competitive intelligence company on June 11, 2026.
To that end, organizations will be unable to connect to Salesforce via the app until further notice, the American cloud-based software company noted in an alert published this week.
"Salesforce took
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Confidence Lacks in Threat Detection Across Non-Email Channels like Slack and Teams
item.description: Half of cybersecurity leaders lack confidence in detecting threats on Slack, Teams and other non-email platforms, despite growing attacker focus

item.title: Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk’s Agentic SOC
item.description: <p>WideField will accelerate Agentic SOC capabilities by expanding the lens on threat investigation to include identity, credentials, sessions, and blast radius.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cisco-to-acquire-widefield-security-to-boost-splunks-agentic-soc/">Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk&#8217;s Agentic SOC</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: 15,000 WordPress Websites Cleaned Up in SocGholish Botnet Takedown
item.description: <p>Law enforcement and private partners took down 106 SocGholish C&#038;C servers and domains as part of Operation Endgame.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/15000-wordpress-websites-cleaned-up-in-socgholish-botnet-takedown/">15,000 WordPress Websites Cleaned Up in SocGholish Botnet Takedown </a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Apple Patches Beats Studio Buds Flaw Letting Nearby Attackers Spy via Microphone
item.description:
Apple has updated its Beats Studio Buds wireless earbuds to patch a high-severity vulnerability that could be exploited by nearby hackers to eavesdrop on users.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2025-20701 (CVSS score: 8.8), refers to a case of incorrect authorization impacting the Airoha Bluetooth audio SDK that makes it possible to pair a Bluetooth audio device without user consent.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure
item.description: <p>CISA has given federal agencies only three days to patch CVE-2026-20253, which can be exploited for unauthenticated remote code execution.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/splunk-enterprise-vulnerability-exploited-in-attacks-days-after-disclosure/">Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: 24B Records Exposed in Massive Leak of Emails, Passwords, and Login Data
item.description: <p>Cybernews researchers found an exposed database with 24 billion credential records, raising fresh risks from password reuse and credential stuffing.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-24-billion-credential-records-exposed-database/">24B Records Exposed in Massive Leak of Emails, Passwords, and Login Data</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: 124M Passwords Exposed as Infostealer Malware Hits Millions of Devices
item.description: <p>Have I Been Pwned has added 124 million passwords and 56 million email addresses from infostealer logs tied to infected devices.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-have-i-been-pwned-infostealer-passwords-124m/">124M Passwords Exposed as Infostealer Malware Hits Millions of Devices</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
item.description: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. This week, researchers from multiple security firms concluded that the Popa botnet is linked to NetNut, a "residential proxy" provider operated by the publicly-traded Israeli firm Alarum Technologies Ltd [NASDAQ: ALAR].
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: F5 Patches Two Critical NGINX Open Source Flaws Enabling Remote Code Execution
item.description:
F5 has released security updates to address two critical security flaws in NGINX Open Source that could be exploited to achieve code execution on affected systems.
The vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2026-42530 (CVSS v4 score: 9.2) - A use-after-free vulnerability in the ngx_http_v3_module that could be triggered by a remote unauthenticated attacker when NGINX Open Source is
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Majority of Internet-Accessible REDCap Servers Outdated
item.description: <p>These servers are regularly targeted by China-linked UNC6508 for initial access and backdoor deployment.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/majority-of-internet-accessible-redcap-servers-outdated/">Majority of Internet-Accessible REDCap Servers Outdated</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: AI Crackdowns, Mega Mergers, and Security Chaos Define This Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from June 15–18.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-crackdowns-mega-mergers-and-security-chaos-define-this-week-in-tech/">AI Crackdowns, Mega Mergers, and Security Chaos Define This Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Orphaned AI Agents: How to Find Hidden Access Risks Inside Your Network
item.description:
If an autonomous AI agent interacts with your company's core intellectual property today, can your security team instantly name the person who authorized it?
For most enterprises, the answer is a simple no.
The rush to adopt internal AI tools has left a massive trail of administrative debt: orphaned agents (AI tools left running after their creator leaves the company) and standing privileges (
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Claude Chat Abuse, NastyC2 npm Packages, Device-Code Phishing + 25 More Stories
item.description:
The internet did not break this week. It got used exactly as designed, which is worse.
Searches were siphoned through shady browser add-ons. AI chat links turned into malware delivery paths. macOS attacks ran in memory and left almost nothing behind. Cloud agents looked like helpers until attackers treated them like open shells.
Add exposed edge gear, poisoned packages, cash courier scams,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fake GitHub Stars and AI Videos Mask a Crypto Clipper
item.description: A Rust crypto clipper hides behind fake GitHub stars and AI-narrated YouTube videos

item.title: ICO Cautions Healthcare Worker After Princess of Wales Incident
item.description: Hospital insider escapes criminal prosecution after attempting to sell royal’s medical records

item.title: Microsoft Details Windows Clipper Malware Campaign Using USB LNK Worm and Tor-Based C2
item.description: Microsoft has disclosed details of a Windows-based cryptocurrency clipper campaign that has targeted users since February 2026 with clipboard-intercepting malware with self-spreading capabilities and using the Tor anonymity network to hide communication. "The clipper in this campaign relies on Windows Script Host and ActiveX-driven logic to launch a bundled Tor proxy and poll a hidden-service C2
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: INC Ransomware Emerges as Major RaaS Threat in 2026 with 830+ Victims Since 2023
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have charted the evolution of INC from an nascent ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation to one of the most prolific cybercrime groups in 2026, claiming no less than 830 victims since August 2023.
"The disruption of LockBit and the shutdown of BlackCat created opportunities for INC to expand as affiliates migrated to alternative ransomware operations," Acronis
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Scripts on Your Checkout Page Are Now a PCI DSS Problem
item.description:
An independent PCI assessor tested Reflectiz against the new PCI DSS rules. Here is the verdict: See the full QSA assessment here →
When a customer types their card number into your checkout, their browser is running far more than your code. Analytics tags, a tag manager, a support widget, a payment iframe: a modern checkout loads dozens of third-party scripts, and any one of them can be turned
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: DragonForce Hackers Abuse Microsoft Teams Relays to Hide Backdoor.Turn C2 Traffic
item.description:
Threat actors associated with the DragonForce ransomware have been observed using a custom Go-based remote access trojan (RAT) called Backdoor.Turn to conceal command-and-control (C2) traffic inside Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure.
According to findings from Broadcom-owned Symantec and Carbon Black, the backdoor was deployed against a major U.S. services firm. The name of the company was
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Accenture to Acquire Majority Stake in Dragos, All of runZero, NetRise in $4.1 Billion OT Cybersecurity Push
item.description: <p>The deal values industrial cybersecurity giant Dragos at $3.25 billion, and runZero and NetRise will operate under Dragos.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/accenture-to-acquire-majority-stake-in-dragos-all-of-runzero-netrise-in-4-1-billion-ot-cybersecurity-push/">Accenture to Acquire Majority Stake in Dragos, All of runZero, NetRise in $4.1 Billion OT Cybersecurity Push</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Cybercriminals Are Worried About AI Taking Their Jobs Too
item.description: Analysis of chatter on underground forums by Sophos finds that hackers fear AI could take work away from them

item.title: No Exploits Required
item.description: <p>Four decades of incident response experience suggest that exploits are often the symptom, not the root cause, of today’s cybersecurity failures.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/no-exploits-required/">No Exploits Required</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tod Beardsley

item.title: Dream Raises $260 Million at $3 Billion Valuation
item.description: <p>The Israeli startup provides sovereign AI and cyber defenses for governments and critical infrastructure.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/dream-raises-260-million-at-3-billion-valuation/">Dream Raises $260 Million at $3 Billion Valuation</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: LATAM Infrastructure Hit by Fortinet and Ivanti Exploits
item.description: CloudSEK maps Operation Escaneo, a campaign hitting Latin American infrastructure via perimeter bugs

item.title: Embedding Forbidden Text in Spyware to Discourage AI Analysis
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Killing me gently: Inside Gentlemen’s EDR killer framework
item.description: ESET Research shares the results of a months-long investigation into the suite of EDR killers maintained by the RaaS gang Gentlemen

item.title: Hostile States Behind 75% of Cyber-Attacks on UK Critical Infrastructure, NCSC Warns
item.description: Richard Horne, the NCSC CEO, said three-quarters of cyber-attacks targeting UK critical infrastructure came from nation-state actors

item.title: Cybercrime Surges in APAC as Digitalization Takes Hold
item.description: Interpol claims cybercrime accounts for third of crime in over half of Asia and South Pacific countries

item.title: What Is an RFP Response? A Guide for Security and GRC Teams | UpGuard
item.description: Stop letting the RFP security section bottleneck your sales. Learn how GRC teams can streamline response workflows, map controls, and close deals faster

item.title: Crypto Clipper Campaign Abuses Fake Reviews, AI Narrators, and VirusTotal Comments
item.description:
An unknown threat actor has been observed leveraging paid or promoted posts on legitimate news websites to drum up buzz for their warez, according to new findings from Check Point Research.
The threat actor also has at their disposal a dedicated WordPress phishing page that acts as the central hub, alongside GitHub and SourceForge projects promoted by fake accounts, a YouTube channel, and a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 105K Chrome Installs Linked to Adware and Fake Google Traffic
item.description: <p>Socket researchers linked 152 Chrome wallpaper extensions to hidden data logging, fake Google search traffic, and ad monetization.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-chrome-extensions-fake-traffic-user-data/">105K Chrome Installs Linked to Adware and Fake Google Traffic</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
item.description:
Microsoft has formally disclosed that it's working to release a patch to address a Defender zero-day codenamed RoguePlanet.
The vulnerability has now been assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-50656 (CVSS score: 7.8), with the tech giant describing it as a privilege escalation flaw.
"Microsoft is aware of an elevation of privilege in the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine in Microsoft Defender
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Confirms Security Incident After $25M Hacker Demand
item.description: <p>Hackers claim they stole 1.3TB of Novo Nordisk data, including clinical trial and AI model information, after issuing a $25 million demand.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-novo-nordisk-1-3tb-theft-25m-demand-emea/">Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Confirms Security Incident After $25M Hacker Demand</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Junior Hacker Used Tailscale and OpenSSH to Keep Access After His C2 Went Offline
item.description:
A French-speaking attacker broke into a small French automotive business, planted a keylogger, and stole banking and email credentials.
Ordinary stuff, until one move near the end.
Before his command-and-control server went dark, he installed OpenSSH and Tailscale on a victim's machine, building a way back in that did not run through the C2 at all. When the Havoc server went offline the next
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: North Korean Hiring Fraud Runs on AI and US Laptop Farms
item.description: Nisos infiltrated a North Korean IT-worker fraud cell running on AI interviews and a US laptop farm

item.title: Adversarial Exposure Validation Turns Security Visibility into Confident Prioritization
item.description:
For security teams, the findings never stop, but confidence in knowing which ones matter is becoming harder to maintain.
The problem is no longer visibility. It's validation. Security teams must decide which findings warrant action while operating under constant pressure and incomplete information. Increasingly, the challenge is not discovering potential risks. It is determining which risks
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Serverless Phishing Kit on GitHub Targets Mexican Banks
item.description: GitBait phishing kit abuses GitHub Pages and the SheetBest API to steal Mexican banking credentials

item.title: Malicious JetBrains Plugins Steal AI API Keys as Chrome Extensions Capture Chatbot Chats
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a "coordinated malware campaign" on the JetBrains Marketplace that has published no less than 15 malicious plugins capable of exfiltrating artificial intelligence (AI) provider keys.
"Every plugin poses as an AI coding assistant built on DeepSeek and other large language models, offering chat, commit messages, code review, bug finding, and unit tests,"
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Adds New Android Controls for WhatsApp Backups, Password Transfers
item.description: <p>Google’s June 2026 Android system updates add WhatsApp backup controls, Play Protect checks, passkey portability, and Play Store AI search.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-android-june-2026-system-updates/">Google Adds New Android Controls for WhatsApp Backups, Password Transfers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Sensitive Enterprise Data Uploads to AI Models Double in a Year
item.description: The rise of AI-assistants and applications in the enterprise has seen a 93% increase in employees attempting to upload sensitive data, bringing security challenges

item.title: AI Threats and Alert Fatigue Challenge Cybersecurity Teams
item.description: Filigran survey at Infosecurity Europe 2026 reveals AI-powered attacks as the top concern, with false positives, alert fatigue and manual processes draining security teams

item.title: AI Use by the US Government
item.description: <p>On 14 April, the Trump administration quietly acknowledged the widespread use of AI to automate government processes. The office of management and budget (OMB) <a href="https://github.com/ombegov/2025-Federal-Agency-AI-Use-Case-Inventory/commit/3c225ba8438e48306ace7698c8c7feb9486cbc69">disclosed</a> a staggering 3,611 active or planned use cases for AI across the federal government. The list has <a href="https://fedscoop.com/disclosed-government-ai-use-increased-in-2025-omb/">ballooned</a> by 70% from the one published in the final year of the Biden administration, and includes many disturbing-seeming plans to hand over sensitive governmental functions to AI.</p> <p>Scanning this list, many readers may find many causes for alarm. It represents a transfer of decision processes from human to machine on a massive scale over matters of individual freedom, public health and well-being, nuclear reactor safety and more...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: The Top 10 Attack Surface Exposures in 2026
item.description:
Breaches don't always start with a zero-day. An exposed admin panel can get brute-forced, or credentials reused from a previous attack. But when a vulnerability does drop — like MongoBleed earlier this year, which let attackers pull credentials and session tokens from server memory without authentication — anything internet-facing is immediately at risk.
With time-to-exploit now down to a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: EU Security Experts to Support Ukrainian Organizations in Case of Cyber-Attacks
item.description: Ukraine has been added to the EU Cybersecurity Reserve, which provides incident response services against large-scale incidents

item.title: Fifteen JetBrains Marketplace Plugins Found Stealing API Keys
item.description: Aikido Security has discovered at least 15 IDE plugins on the JetBrains Marketplace

item.title: Protecting legacy OT systems against modern cyberthreats
item.description: Many manufacturing plants depend on OT systems that stay in service for many years. That long run can hide significant cybersecurity risks.

item.title: Staffing Is Top SOC Challenge Even as AI Proliferates, Says SANS
item.description: SANS Institute study finds few SOCs have built AI into defined workflows, despite widespread adoption

item.title: 145 Mastra npm Packages Compromised via Hijacked Contributor Account
item.description:
As many as 145 npm packages associated with the Mastra namespace ("@mastra/*"), a popular open-source JavaScript and TypeScript framework for building artificial intelligence (AI) applications, have been compromised as part of a software supply chain attack codenamed easy-day-js, per findings from Endor Labs, JFrog, OX Security, SafeDep, Socket, StepSecurity, and Synk.
"A single npm account (
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Joomla JCE Flaw Allowing PHP Code Execution
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor (JCE) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-48907 (CVSS score: 10.0), is a case of improper access control that could facilitate arbitrary
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Vertex AI SDK Flaw Let Attackers Hijack Model Uploads via Bucket Squatting
item.description:
A flaw in the Google Cloud Vertex AI SDK for Python let an attacker with no access to a victim's project hijack the victim's machine learning model upload and run code inside Google's serving infrastructure.
Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, which found and reported the bug through Google's bug bounty program, calls the technique "Pickle in the Middle" and said it saw no exploitation in the wild.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ClickFix Campaigns Expand Malware Delivery With New Loaders and Fake Update Lures
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged multiple ClickFix campaigns that deliver three malware loaders called BabaDeda Loader, Lorem Ipsum Loader, and Potemkin, per independent reports from Morphisec, BlueVoyant, and Huntress, respectively.
Attacks involving BabaDeda Loader, observed in April 2026, have targeted education and financial organizations.
"Earlier BabaDeda activity was known for
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: SprySOCKS Backdoor Expands From Linux to Windows
item.description: China-linked SprySOCKS backdoor gains stealthy Windows variants and 30-plus C2 commands

item.title: ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe HR Data, Threatens Leak
item.description: <p>ShinyHunters claims it stole 297GB of data from the Council of Europe, including payroll and medical records, but the organization has not confirmed a breach.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-shinyhunters-council-europe-data-theft-claim-emea/">ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe HR Data, Threatens Leak</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Welcome Our Newest Associate Participating Organizations
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Rokarolla Trojan Combines Banking Fraud With Device Surveillance
item.description: Rokarolla Android trojan steals banking logins and spies on victims while blocking fraud alerts

item.title: New Rokarolla Android Malware Steals PINs, SMS Codes, and Crypto Wallet Funds
item.description:
Security researchers at&nbsp;Zimperium's zLabs&nbsp;have documented a new Android banking trojan, Rokarolla, that targets 217 banking and cryptocurrency apps and packs 137 remote commands.
Together, they give an operator near-total control of an infected phone: it lifts lock-screen PINs, reads and sends SMS, rewrites the clipboard to redirect crypto payments, and switches off Google Play
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Over Two-Thirds of Security Pros Say Cyber Is Getting Harder
item.description: ISSA study finds most security professionals feel challenged by colleagues’ involvement in cyber

item.title: Survey: 94% of Incidents Involve Anonymized Infrastructure. Teams Are Still Reactive
item.description:
Security teams have never had more IP data at their disposal. Every day, analysts ingest enrichment feeds, geolocation data, reputation scores, telemetry, and threat intelligence from a growing ecosystem of vendors and platforms.
Yet despite this abundance of information, many organizations continue to face a fundamental challenge: sifting through the noise to understand who is behind an IP and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: DragonForce Ransomware Exploited Microsoft Teams to Hide in Attack Against Major Company
item.description: Command and control traffic exploited a Teams visitor token to make malicious activity look legitimate to defenders

item.title: Flock Cameras Are Being Used for Stalking
item.description: <p>There are over a dozen cases around the country where police officers are using the Flock surveillance camera system to obsessively and illegally <a href="https://www.404media.co/cops-keep-getting-arrested-for-using-flock-to-stalk-people/">stalk people</a>.</p> <p><a href="https://archive.ph/l5KcH">Alternate link</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Chainguard, JPMorgan, BNY Team Up to Secure Open Source from AI Threats
item.description: Athena is a new an industry coalition to fix the vulnerabilities frontier AI models find before attackers can exploit them

item.title: Attackers Exploit Three Fortinet FortiSandbox Flaws, One Patched Last Week
item.description:
Bad actors are exploiting multiple security vulnerabilities in Fortinet FortiSandbox, according to threat intelligence firm Defused Cyber.
In a post shared on X, the company said it has observed exploitation of CVE-2026-39813, CVE-2026-39808, and CVE-2026-25089 over the past 24 hours.
CVE-2026-39813 (CVSS score: 9.1) refers to a path traversal vulnerability in FortiSandbox JRPC API that could
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: China-Linked SprySOCKS Backdoor Expands to Windows with Driver-Based Stealth
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two previously undocumented Windows variants of what was believed to be a Linux-only backdoor called SprySOCKS.
"The Windows variants discovered are internally marked as WIN_DRV and WIN_PLUS," ESET said in a report shared with The Hacker News. "Both come with a hard-coded C&amp;C [command-and-control] configuration and support communication over TCP, UDP,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: FishMonger’s arsenal upgraded: SprySOCKS for Windows
item.description: ESET researchers have discovered SprySOCKS for Windows, FishMonger’s backdoor weaponizing a kernel driver for advanced stealthiness

item.title: FBI Warns Courier Cash Pickups Are Driving Crypto Scams
item.description: The FBI claims couriers are being used to circumvent bank transfers in crypto investment schemes

item.title: Fake Microsoft Alerts Used to Deploy North Korean NarwhalRAT Malware
item.description:
The North Korean state-sponsored hacking group known as ScarCruft (aka APT37) has been observed using spear-phishing messages impersonating Microsoft Account security notifications to deliver a new malware called NarwhalRAT.
"The attack email contained a message impersonating an MS account security alert," the Genians Security Center (GSC) said. "It was designed to create concern over possible
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cisco Releases Security Updates for Actively Exploited SD-WAN Manager Flaw
item.description:
Cisco has released security updates for a medium-severity security flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager that has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20262, carries a CVSS score of 6.5 out of 10.0.
"A vulnerability in the web UI of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly SD-WAN vManage, could allow an authenticated, remote attacker to create a file or
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Flags LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Flaw Exploited for Root Privilege Escalation
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a security flaw impacting LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the fixes by June 18, 2026.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-54420 (CVSS score: 8.5), which has been described as a case of privilege
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Nintendo Alleged Data Breach: Threat Actor Demands $2M Ransom
item.description: <p>Nintendo faces an alleged data extortion incident involving HR records, internal reports, and potential exposure of third-party vendors.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-nintendo-alleged-data-leak-third-party-risk/">Nintendo Alleged Data Breach: Threat Actor Demands $2M Ransom</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
item.description:
A China-linked espionage group hid inside North American medical, academic, and military research networks for more than a year, quietly stealing sensitive research and defense email.
The way in was a backdoor on their REDCap research servers that stole login credentials. The exfiltration was the unusual part: the attackers rewired the victims' own Google Workspace rules to copy any message
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: North Korean Hackers Are Turning Developer Tools Into Malware Delivery Channels
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two malicious cyber campaigns that exhibit similarities with a persistent North Korean threat cluster known as Contagious Interview (aka Famous Chollima, HexagonalRodent, and Void Dokkaebi).
According to a report published by Proofpoint, the threat actor has been found orchestrating phishing campaigns using developer role recruitment or code review themes
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Attackers Hijack Popular WordPress Plugins to Deploy Backdoors
item.description: Tampered OptinMonster and sister plugins plant hidden backdoors on 1.2 million WordPress sites

item.title: LiteLLM Vulnerability Chain Lets Low-Privilege Users Take Over AI Gateway Servers
item.description:
A default low-privilege account on a LiteLLM proxy can climb to full admin and run code on the server by chaining three vulnerabilities, researchers at Obsidian Security disclosed
LiteLLM is a widely deployed open-source AI gateway that brokers calls to more than 100 model providers behind one OpenAI-compatible interface.
A server takeover exposes every provider key it holds, the secrets that
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Adriatic Port Cyber-Attack by Anubis Sparks Warning Over Maritime Security Risks
item.description: How the Anubis ransomware group stole and leaked an Italian Adriatic port authority's data

item.title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
item.description:
A single click on a trusted Microsoft link could have let an attacker pull emails, calendar details, and indexed files out of Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search.
Researchers at Varonis Threat Labs chained three bugs into a one-click exfiltration path they call SearchLeak. Because the link pointed to a real microsoft.com domain, traditional anti-phishing and URL filtering tools were
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Bug Hunter Claims $500K From AI-Assisted Vulnerability Pipeline
item.description: <p>A researcher known as Brutecat says an AI-assisted pipeline helped map and test more than 1,500 Google APIs. Google has not confirmed the $500,000 payout total.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-ai-bug-bounty-pipeline/">Google Bug Hunter Claims $500K From AI-Assisted Vulnerability Pipeline</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Chrome 0-Day, UniFi Exploits, macOS Stealers, VPN Flaw and More
item.description:
Stuff broke again. Not in a movie way. An old tool was left exposed. An abandoned package was abused. A deprecated feature was still running in prod.
This week is the same lesson in a new form: phishing kits are easier to rent, AI names are useful bait, old login paths still fail, and forgotten software keeps becoming someone else's entry point.
Scroll through the full Monday Cybersecurity
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Oracle Warns PeopleSoft Customers After Critical Zero-Day Exploited
item.description: <p>Oracle issued emergency guidance for CVE-2026-35273, a critical PeopleSoft flaw exploited in a ShinyHunters-linked campaign targeting universities.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-oracle-peoplesoft-zero-day-shinyhunters/">Oracle Warns PeopleSoft Customers After Critical Zero-Day Exploited</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: The Onboarding Password Mistake That Creates Unnecessary Risk
item.description:
Employee onboarding is a busy time for IT teams. New starters need devices, accounts, access permissions, and passwords, all delivered within a tight timeframe.
That usually means sharing a temporary "first-day" password so employees can access systems for the first time. The issue is that these passwords don't always stay temporary. They may be sent over email or SMS, reused across accounts,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 152 Chrome Wallpaper Extensions with 105K Installs Linked to Adware and Fake Traffic
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a network of 152 Google Chrome extensions that act as new tab live wallpaper add-ons to distribute a potentially unwanted program (PUP) family.
The cluster spans 38 separate Chrome Web Store publisher accounts and three brand backends: tabplugins[.]com, yowgames[.]com, and chromewallpaper[.]com. They have been collectively installed 105,000 times. The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The FCC Wants to Eliminate Burner Phones
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Cybersecurity Experts Urge US to Lift Ban on Anthropic's Frontier AI Models
item.description: Access to two Anthropic large language models, Mythos 5 and Fable 5, has effectively been banned to any non-US nationals by the Trump administration

item.title: Popular WordPress Plugin Scripts Tampered to Plant Hidden Backdoors on Sites
item.description:
An attacker tampered with trusted JavaScript files used by WordPress sites running PushEngage, OptinMonster, and TrustPulse, turning those files into a way to break into the sites.
When a site administrator was logged in as the file loaded, the code created an admin account under the attacker's control and installed a hidden plugin that opened a way back in. Ordinary visitors did not trigger it
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UK Government Finds 400+ Vulnerabilities in AI Hackathons
item.description: Government departments find hundreds of vulnerabilities after testing frontier models

item.title: Maine Takes Breach Reporting Portal Offline After Fake Entries
item.description: The Office of the Maine Attorney General has suspended its breach reporting portal

item.title: EvilTokens: A phishing attack that doesn’t steal your password
item.description: A phishing kit subverting Microsoft’s legitimate authentication flow lets attackers break into accounts without stealing passwords or creating fake login pages

item.title: Sniper Dz Scams Target MENA Users via Fake Facebook Offers and Browser Alerts
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of fraudulent activity targeting users across the Middle East and North Africa by employing various fraudulent Facebook accounts impersonating politicians, public figures, and trusted organizations.
"These accounts promoted fake offers, including free mobile internet packages, financial compensation, and government subsidy programs," Group-IB
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Palo Alto Warns of Active Exploitation of PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN Flaw
item.description:
Palo Alto Networks has revealed that it has observed "active exploitation" of a recently disclosed PAN-OS vulnerability by an unknown threat actor to obtain unauthorized access to GlobalProtect portals.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), an authentication bypass flaw affecting the portal and gateway components of PAN-OS software that could be exploited by bad
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
item.description:
Splunk has released security updates to address a critical security flaw in Splunk Enterprise that could be exploited to conduct unauthenticated file operations and even remote code execution.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20253, is rated 9.8 on the CVSS scoring system.
"In Splunk Enterprise versions below 10.2.4 and 10.0.7, an unauthenticated user could create or truncate arbitrary
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
